summary: A historical documentary transcript detailing the transition from the era of monopolies to a new generation of businessmen led by Henry Ford. While it highlights Ford's battle against the ALAM auto cartel and his introduction of the assembly line, it contains very few of Ford's own words or detailed thought processes, resulting in a sparse extraction.

principles.statement: Products should be built for popular use and priced for the common man.
principles.rationale: By making a product like the automobile durable, light, and affordable, it transitions from a luxury item to an everyday utility for the masses.
principles.source_quote: I have set out to build the best motorcar for popular use... which makes it the first car affordable for the common man.

signature_quotes.text: I have set out to build the best motorcar for popular use.
signature_quotes.context: Describing his vision for the Ford Motorcar.

anti_patterns.description: Patenting the broad idea of a product to create a monopoly.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It restricts the product to a single owner, stifling competition and preventing the product from reaching the general public.
